answer:
**Iqbal's poem "Sach keh doon ae Brahman! gar tu bura na maane" (Chapter 36 of *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*) is a direct address to a Brahmin, critiquing idol worship, religious division, and ritualism while calling for unity, love, and a renewed spiritual vision shared across humanity.**[^1]

Here is the full English translation:

> [p-752d60] O Brahmin, if you will not take offense,  
> Your ancient idols have lost reverence.  
> 
> [p-c536e2] From them you learned to hold your kin in scorn;  
> From God, the Preacher learned how war is born.  
> 
> [p-5af363] Fed up at last, I left both mosque and shrine,  
> The Preacher’s words, and those old tales of thine.  
> 
> [p-db56fb] You think your God is in a form of stone;  
> To me, my homeland's every speck is divine.  
> 
> [p-3a582d] Come, let us lift the veils of otherness anew,  
> And wipe away the lines that make us two.  
> 
> [p-425278] The city of the heart lies desolate,  
> Come, in this land a new shrine let's create.  
> 
> [p-948e14] Our pilgrimage must be the world's most high,  
> Its pinnacle should touch the very sky.  
> 
> [p-959af2] Let's rise each dawn to sing the sweetest prayer,  
> And serve the wine of love for all to share.  
> 
> [p-94fb2e] In faithful songs, both strength and peace reside;  
> For all this earth, salvation is love's guide.[^2]

The transliterated Urdu original (Roman script) begins:

> Sach keh doon ae Brahman! gar tu bura na maane  
> Tere sanam-kadon ke but ho gaye purane  
> ... (full text continues as above).[^3]

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, Ch. 36 ("Sach keh doon ae Brahman! gar tu bura na maane"), translation ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/36-sach-keh-doon-ae-brahman-gar-tu-bura-na-maane/translation/](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/36-sach-keh-doon-ae-brahman-gar-tu-bura-na-maane/translation/)).

[^2]: Ibid., full chapter ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/36-sach-keh-doon-ae-brahman-gar-tu-bura-na-maane/translation/](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/36-sach-keh-doon-ae-brahman-gar-tu-bura-na-maane/translation/)). Paragraphs cited via markers in the body.

[^3]: Ibid., transliteration ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/36-sach-keh-doon-ae-brahman-gar-tu-bura-na-maane/transliteration/](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/36-sach-keh-doon-ae-brahman-gar-tu-bura-na-maane/transliteration/)).
